mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy expectations, increasing equity discount rates and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during periods of rising CPIAUCSL, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in PAYEMS exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic activity that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS rises above the 200,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST indicate weakening consumer spending and housing demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive or neutral when industrial production falls below trend during high inflation periods, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level exceeding 2.0% triggers immediate repricing of growth equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week as discount rates rise sharply.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations where the federal funds rate surpasses 2.0%, negating the valuation compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE reduces household consumption capacity and increases recession probability, systematically lowering S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rising unemployment rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDPC1 and falling RSAFS indicates income growth is not translating to consumer spending, creating a demand shock that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative monthly returns, with predictive accuracy indistinguishable from random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in INDPRO above 0.5% signals strengthening manufacturing output that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where industrial production rises above 0.5% during recovery phases.
